decision_description: Constitutional violation detected under CLAUDE.md §13.3 in docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase7_budget_gate/budget_gate_assessment.json. The artifact names eight specific project partner identifiers (ATU, CERIA, BIIS, ISIA, NIHS, ELI, BAL, FIIT) and nine work package identifiers (WP1–WP9) as project facts but contains no traceability_footer.primary_sources[] field referencing docs/tier3_project_instantiation/ to ground these identifiers in Tier 3 source data. Additionally, the budget_response_reference field holds the value 'budget_response_placeholder.json'; the presence of 'placeholder' in the filename raises critical concern that the budget coverage assertions recorded in the artifact — all eight partners present_in_budget: true, all nine WPs present_in_budget: true, blocking_inconsistencies: [] — may not be grounded in a genuine validated response received from the external Lump Sum Budget Planner system through the docs/integrations/lump_sum_budget_planner/received/ channel, constituting potential fabrication of budget-consistency project facts in violation of CLAUDE.md §13.3 and §8.3. Human operator resolution is required to verify: (1) that the referenced budget response is a genuine external system response, not a fabricated placeholder; (2) that the partner and WP identifiers in the assessment are traceable to Tier 3 consortium and WP design data; and (3) whether the gate_pass_declaration of 'pass' is constitutionally valid given the placeholder budget reference.
tier_authority_applied: CLAUDE.md §13.3 — prohibition on inventing project facts (partner names, capabilities, roles, objectives, prior experience, budget figures, team sizes, equipment) not present in Tier 3; CLAUDE.md §8.3 — no agent may invent, substitute, approximate, or silently generate budget figures; budget computation is exclusively the responsibility of the external Lump Sum Budget Planner system; CLAUDE.md §8.4 — the budget gate cannot be bypassed or substituted with internally generated budget estimates
rationale: The constitutional-compliance-check skill audited docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase7_budget_gate/budget_gate_assessment.json under run_id 516af107-14ae-4baf-8fa5-4553cb2b7441 and identified a critical-severity violation of CLAUDE.md §13.3. The artifact records specific partner_ids and wp_ids as project facts with no traceability_footer.primary_sources[] references to Tier 3 source documents. The budget_response_reference value 'budget_response_placeholder.json' indicates that the budget coverage claims — which form the direct evidential basis for the gate_pass_declaration of 'pass' — may not derive from a genuine validated response received through the canonical integration channel. CLAUDE.md §8.1 declares that budget computation is exclusively the responsibility of the external Lump Sum Budget Planner system. CLAUDE.md §8.3 prohibits any agent from inventing, substituting, approximating, or silently generating budget figures. CLAUDE.md §8.4 states that Phase 7 is mandatory and cannot be bypassed, deferred, or substituted with internally generated budget estimates. A placeholder budget response that is not a genuine external system output does not satisfy the budget gate condition. The violation is recorded as unresolved per CLAUDE.md §9.4 and §12.3: it requires explicit human operator verification before the gate_pass_declaration of 'pass' can be treated as constitutionally valid, and no Phase 8 activity may commence until this violation is resolved.
